[97:10]You all saw it, I'm sure.

[97:25]about the message, about the optics of it.

[97:31]I wanna do it with respect to Charlie Kirk,

[97:35]a man who has passed.

[97:36]his family, his friends, who I'm sure are grieving.

[97:42]But this is politics,

[97:45]and this is a big part of politics.

[97:48]And by that, I mean, we see over and over and over,

[97:53]time and time again.

[97:57]Sentiment, emotions, crisis,

[98:01]These are the extraordinary circumstances

[98:08]that politicians traffic in.

[98:11]We all have a sense of identity,

[98:14]and we all have an intentional idea of what we support

[98:19]and our values and our beliefs.

[98:22]We all think we know who we are and what we're about

[98:26]until a catastrophe happens.

[98:29]until a crisis happens.

[98:32]And then everybody's emotions skyrocket

[98:36]and sentiment is cranked up.

[98:41]And whereas ordinarily we think we know who we are

[98:45]and we think we know what we're about

[98:47]and what we support and what we believe in.

[98:50]It is in extraordinary times

[98:53]when we tend to get dragged along.

[98:55]into things that maybe ordinarily we wouldn't support

[98:59]and maybe ordinarily we wouldn't go with.

[99:04]And that is because when tragedies

[99:07]and catastrophes happen, when fear, panic,

[99:11]and other emotions come in, it makes us influenceable.

[99:17]It makes us easy to manipulate.

[99:20]And it makes us easy to manipulate

[99:21]because certain things are raised up

[99:25]which are beyond reproach,

[99:27]which people believe are beyond criticism.

[99:31]Some things are off limits and protected.

[99:34]And that's how that works.

[99:37]And so the reason I say all this

[99:39]and the reason I lay that out, maybe that sounds strange,

[99:42]I think this is one of these moments.

[99:45]And we talk a lot on the show about false flags,

[99:48]about things like 9-11, about October 7th.

[99:52]And

[99:58]I'm unconvinced that it's some type of operation, but like false flags, like these other manufactured

[100:14]very quickly move in to take advantage and push an agenda.

[100:21]that, that was on full display. Whatever you think about the tragedy that took place two

[102:51]last week with President Trump

[104:00]about what he would do with the program.

[104:02]Silicon Valley wants limitless H-1B visas.

[104:06]They want an unlimited number of these temporary visas

[104:10]to be given out to mostly Indians,

[104:13]as well as other Asian migrant workers.

[104:58]per visa to $100,000 per year.

[105:03]This is huge because if you understand the problem

[105:07]with H1B, the problem is it makes foreign labor

[105:25]on their employer for their visa, and for their residency inside the country.

[150:51]is we have to understand it in principle,

[150:54]that it is always in extraordinary circumstances

[151:00]that revolutions occur.

[151:02]It is always under extraordinary circumstances

[151:06]that a regime will exploit the passions,

[151:10]the mood,

[151:11]the mob, of the moment, to aggressively push an agenda that under normal

[151:55]These aggressive agendas are pushed through with propaganda by hijacking

[152:01]Feelings hijacking emotion when reason is suspended and this is no different than any other thing

[152:56]It's in the moment when propaganda and emotions cloud everything.

[153:05]that come into play, it's in the moment in confusion

[153:19]or aggressive tyrannical kind of thing,

[153:22]in order for us to prevent that in the future,

[153:24]we have to be mature and we have to look at it

[153:26]in the moment, not in the past.

[167:55]The world will die.
